subject: BUG: Invalid longitude values causing API failures
body:
Sarah,

I found a frontend issue causing API failures. The browser console shows:

```
longitude=-210.19043
longitude=-220.78125
```

These are outside the valid range of -180 to +180. This happens when users pan the map past the International Date Line.

**APIs Affected:**
- /api/masteredLocations/nearestMastered (was returning 503, now returns 400)
- /api/venues (returns 400)
- /api/events (returns 400)

**Suggested Fix:**
Normalize longitude before sending to API:

```javascript
function normalizeLongitude(lng) {
  // Wrap longitude to -180 to 180 range
  let normalized = ((lng + 180) % 360) - 180;
  if (normalized < -180) normalized += 360;
  return normalized;
}
```

Apply this to any coordinates from map controls or geolocation before API calls.

**Backend Status:**
I've added coordinate validation to nearestMastered. It now returns 400 with a clear error instead of crashing with 503.

—Fulton
